idea.title: Agent Budget Triage
idea.hook: You have $5 of API budget and a queue of 20 dev tasks — assign each task to the cheap Haiku, mid Sonnet, or expensive Opus agent without burning your runway before the milestone.
idea.prototype: Vertical task queue (20 cards). Three model lanes at bottom with cost/quality stats. Tap task -> tap lane. Each task has hidden 'difficulty' — cheap model on hard task = retry cost; expensive model on trivial task = budget drain. Milestone = clear queue before budget hits $0. Daily seed = task difficulty distribution.
idea.why_novel: Per-TASK model routing (Haiku/Sonnet/Opus) vs per-token; meta about the actual decision Tim makes daily routing work in this factory
idea.non_timer_pressure: Dollar budget ($5 hard cap) + queue depletion — no timer, only spend-pressure
idea.lose_condition: Budget drops to $0 with tasks still in queue

sources.title: Agent cost economics
sources.snippet: agent cost/economics matters more than raw model quality

synthesizer_brief: Resource allocation puzzle about AI agent cost/quality trade-off
